q.en: Why is Leo reluctant to leave the city and visit his father's girlfriend Lucinda?
q.zh: 莱奥为什么不愿意离开城市去见父亲的新女友露辛达？

options.en: He loves the forest and wants to stay there according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他热爱森林，想留在那里（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He is afraid of his father
options.zh: 他害怕父亲

options.en: He has to go to summer school
options.zh: 他必须去上暑期班

options.en: His parents are divorced and he wishes they were still together, so he resents the new situation
options.zh: 他的父母离了婚，他希望他们仍在一起，因此抗拒这种新状况

explain.en: The book says Leo 'did not want to leave the city' and 'wished his parents were still together--that they hadn't divorced,' feeling resigned about meeting his father's new girlfriend Lucinda.
explain.zh: 书中说莱奥“不想离开城市”，并“希望父母仍在一起——他们没有离婚”，对去见父亲的新女友露辛达感到无奈。

q.en: Who is Dontrelle, and how is he connected to Leo?
q.zh: 唐特雷尔是谁？他和莱奥有什么关系？

options.en: The family's dog
options.zh: 家里的狗

options.en: Leo's teacher
options.zh: 莱奥的老师

options.en: Leo's older brother according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 莱奥的哥哥（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Lucinda's son, who is also in the fifth grade, so the boys are the same age
options.zh: 露辛达的儿子，也在读五年级，所以两个男孩同龄

explain.en: Thomas tells Leo: 'Lucinda has a son in the fifth grade, too. His name is Dontrelle,' making him a same-age boy Leo is meant to befriend.
explain.zh: 托马斯告诉莱奥：“露辛达也有个读五年级的儿子，叫唐特雷尔。”这意味着两人同龄，是理应成为朋友的孩子。

q.en: After the boys get lost, how do they figure out which direction will lead them home?
q.zh: 两个男孩迷路后，是如何判断该朝哪个方向才能回家的？

options.en: Dontrelle finds the North Star to know north, and Leo recalls the sun set in front of them (west), so they walk east
options.zh: 唐特雷尔找到北极星确定北方，莱奥想起太阳是在他们前方落下的（西），于是向东走

options.en: They call their parents on a phone
options.zh: 他们用电话联系父母

options.en: They follow the fox
options.zh: 他们跟着狐狸走

options.en: They follow a road sign to the city according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 他们跟着路标回到城市（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

explain.en: Dontrelle uses Polaris (the North Star) to find north; Leo remembers his science teacher said the sun 'sets in the west,' so since they walked west toward the sunset, they must head east to return.
explain.zh: 唐特雷尔用北极星确定北方；莱奥记得科学老师说过太阳“在西边落下”，既然他们是朝夕阳（西）走的，回家就得向东。

q.en: Near the end, Dontrelle points out a constellation named after Leo. How does this affect Leo?
q.zh: 临近结尾，唐特雷尔指出有一个以“莱奥”命名的星座。这对莱奥产生了什么影响？

options.en: It makes Leo want to become an astronomer
options.zh: 这让他想当天文学家

options.en: It scares Leo even more
options.zh: 这把莱奥吓得更厉害

options.en: Learning about 'Leo the Lion' helps Leo feel brave, and he later walks calmly past the fox like a fearless lion
options.zh: 了解“狮子莱奥”星座让莱奥鼓起勇气，后来他像无畏的狮子一样从容走过狐狸身边

options.en: It makes Leo go back to the city immediately according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 这让他立刻返回城市（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

explain.en: Dontrelle tells Leo 'Leo the Lion' is a constellation and that the lion 'feared nothing'; Leo then feels he 'could be brave like the lion' and later walks toward the fox, who runs away. Dontrelle calls him 'the fearless lion.'
explain.zh: 唐特雷尔告诉莱奥“狮子莱奥”是个星座，而狮子“无所畏惧”；莱奥于是觉得自己“能像狮子一样勇敢”，后来走向狐狸，狐狸逃开。唐特雷尔称他是“无畏的狮子”。

q.en: The narrator describes Leo's thoughts and feelings but not the private thoughts of other characters. What point of view is this?
q.zh: 叙述者描写了莱奥的想法和感受，却没有描写其他角色的内心想法。这是哪种视角？

options.en: Thomas's diary entries
options.zh: 托马斯的日记

options.en: Third-person narration focused mainly on Leo
options.zh: 主要聚焦莱奥的第三人称叙述

options.en: First-person 'I' told by Leo himself according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 莱奥本人的第一人称“我”（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Told by the fox
options.zh: 由狐狸讲述

explain.en: The story uses a third-person narrator who shows Leo's inner feelings (his resentment, fear, relief) while keeping other characters' private thoughts off the page.
explain.zh: 故事采用第三人称叙述者，展现莱奥的内心感受（怨恨、恐惧、宽慰），却不揭示其他角色的内心想法。

q.en: The title 'Leo the Lion' is more than a name. What does the lion symbolize in the story?
q.zh: 标题“狮子莱奥”不只是一个名字。在故事中，狮子象征着什么？

options.en: A book Dontrelle is reading according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 唐特雷尔在读的一本书（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Leo's growth from a frightened boy into a brave one
options.zh: 莱奥从一个害怕的男孩成长为勇敢的人

options.en: An actual lion at the zoo
options.zh: 动物园里真正的一头狮子

options.en: The city Leo lives in
options.zh: 莱奥居住的城市

explain.en: The constellation Leo the Lion, described as fearless with impenetrable skin, mirrors Leo's character change; by the end he acts like 'the fearless lion.'
explain.zh: 狮子莱奥星座被描述为无所畏惧、皮肤刀枪不入，映照了莱奥的性格转变；结尾他表现得像“无畏的狮子”。

q.en: The story teaches real astronomy to move the plot. Which real facts does Dontrelle use to navigate?
q.zh: 故事借助真实的天文学推动情节。唐特雷尔用了哪些真实的天文知识来辨别方向？

options.en: The phases of the moon show direction according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 月相指示方向（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: Polaris (the North Star) sits in the Little Dipper and always points north; the Big Dipper's cup stars line up with it
options.zh: 北极星位于小熊座且总指北方；北斗七星斗柄两端的星与它成一直线

options.en: A comet lights the way
options.zh: 彗星照亮道路

options.en: The planet Mars points south
options.zh: 火星指向南方

explain.en: Dontrelle explains Polaris is 'always in the north' and 'always in the handle of the Little Dipper,' and that the two stars on the end of the Big Dipper's cup align with Polaris.
explain.zh: 唐特雷尔解释北极星“总在北方”，且“总在小熊座的斗柄上”，北斗七星斗勺两端的两颗星与北极星成一直线。

q.en: The boys have very different backgrounds--city versus country. How do their different kinds of knowledge help them?
q.zh: 两个男孩背景迥异——一个来自城市，一个来自乡村。他们不同的知识如何帮到彼此？

options.en: Leo contributes his science-class fact that the sun sets in the west, while Dontrelle contributes star navigation, and together they find the way
options.zh: 莱奥提供了科学课上学到的“太阳西落”，唐特雷尔提供观星辨向，两人合力找到路

options.en: They call the police for help
options.zh: 他们打电话向警察求助

options.en: They both already know how to fish perfectly according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 他们俩本来都很会钓鱼（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: They argue until a ranger finds them
options.zh: 他们一直争吵，直到管理员找到他们

explain.en: Leo remembers the sun sets in the west from science class, and Dontrelle knows the constellations; combining both lets them reason out the route home.
explain.zh: 莱奥从科学课记得太阳西落，唐特雷尔懂得星座；两者结合让他们推理出回家的路线。

q.en: What is a main theme, or message, of the story?
q.zh: 这个故事的主旨或寓意是什么？

options.en: Fear can be overcome and friendship can grow through a shared challenge
options.zh: 恐惧可以被克服，友谊能在共同面对挑战中滋长

options.en: Foxes are dangerous enemies
options.zh: 狐狸是危险的敌人

options.en: Divorce always has a happy ending
options.zh: 离婚总会有美满结局

options.en: City life is always better than country life according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 城市生活总比乡村生活好（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The story shows Leo moving from fear and standoffishness to bravery and a new friendship with Dontrelle, ending with the boys planning more adventures together.
explain.zh: 故事展现莱奥从恐惧、冷淡变为勇敢，并与唐特雷尔建立起新友谊，结尾两个男孩约定再来一次冒险。

q.en: Why does the author include the fox encounter twice--first as a scare, then as a calm passing?
q.zh: 作者为什么让“遇到狐狸”出现两次——先是受惊，后是从容擦肩而过？

options.en: To teach the biology of foxes
options.zh: 为了讲解狐狸的生物学

options.en: To describe a hunting trip
options.zh: 为了描写一次打猎

options.en: To show Leo's character growth from terrified to courageous
options.zh: 为了展现莱奥从惊恐到勇敢的性格成长

options.en: To add a clear villain to the story according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了给故事加一个明确的反派（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The first fox makes Leo bolt in terror; the second time, after learning about Leo the Lion, he walks past it calmly, showing his change--Dontrelle then calls him 'the fearless lion.'
explain.zh: 第一次狐狸让莱奥惊恐逃窜；第二次，在了解“狮子莱奥”后，他从容走过，体现其转变——唐特雷尔于是称他为“无畏的狮子”。

prompt.en: Leo was afraid of the woods at first but learned to be brave. Tell about a time you were scared of something new and how you faced it, or a time you could have been braver.
prompt.zh: 莱奥起初害怕树林，后来学会了勇敢。讲讲你害怕某件新事物并如何应对的经历，或者一次本可以更勇敢的时刻。

en: Describe a specific new or frightening situation, like Leo meeting new people or going somewhere unfamiliar.
Explain what helped you (or could have helped you) be brave, such as a friend's support or learning about the thing you feared.
zh: 描述一个具体的新奇或可怕情境，例如像莱奥那样结识新朋友或去陌生地方。
说明是什么帮助了你（或本可以帮助你）变得勇敢，例如朋友的支持或去了解你所害怕的事物。
